mnemonics: PWD: Present Working Directory. Think 'Print Where I am Directory' to remember it shows your absolute location.
keyPoints: An absolute pathname starts from the root directory (e.g., /home/user/docs)
A relative pathname starts from your current working directory (e.g., docs/report.txt)
The pwd command displays the absolute path of your current working directory
commonMistakes: Confusing relative and absolute paths (e.g., using ./file when /full/path/file is needed)
Forgetting that pwd shows the absolute path, not a relative one
